seed_problem: Frontier-Eng JobShop / ALE-Bench makespan JSSP: emit a full machine schedule; checker validates precedence + no-overlap and scores min(100, 100*optimum/makespan)
mutation: composite (input generalization + theme): generalize the classic job-shop makespan instance to a job-shop with SEQUENCE-DEPENDENT (family-based, asymmetric) setup times on every machine -- the reconfiguration time S[a][b] must elapse between consecutive operations of families a then b on a station -- which makes both the machine no-overlap feasibility check and the makespan objective depend on the chosen ORDER of operations per station, not just their disjointness; skinned as a deep-sea cable splice yard; the score denominator uses a checker-computed always-feasible fully-sequential reference makespan B instead of an unknown optimum
title: Abyssal Splice Yard: Cable Job-Shop with Reconfiguration Setups
strategies: fully-sequential reference schedule (the calibration baseline B): run every operation one at a time in canonical order
SPT dispatch: Giffler-Thompson active-schedule generation breaking machine conflicts by shortest processing time
MWKR dispatch: Giffler-Thompson breaking conflicts by most-work-remaining, refined by a setup-avoidance secondary key that groups same-family operations to save reconfiguration
local search / neighbourhood moves over per-station operation orders (swap adjacent ops, relocate) to jointly cut idle time and setup overhead
